# Discovery Summary

Status: active
Audience: PM, leadership, design, engineering
Owner: ProductOS PM
Updated At: 2026-03-21
Source Artifacts: `current_state_assessment`, `source_note_card`, `research_brief`, `customer_pulse`, `productos_feedback_log`

## Context And Trigger

ProductOS is being self-hosted to reduce PM coordination, reporting, and follow-through overhead while improving trust and operating clarity.

## Evidence Summary

- PM time is being consumed by recurring status reconstruction and coordination chores.
- Discovery and runtime artifacts show that visible queues, structured state, and evidence-backed routing reduce manual reconstruction work.
- Recent feedback also shows that PM communication work still needs a stronger human-readable document layer.

## User And Customer Pain

- PMs lose time reconstructing status and next steps from fragmented sources.
- Stakeholders need readable materials, not just structured data.
- Communication quality drops when evidence exists in JSON but is not packaged into reviewable documents.

## Opportunity Framing

ProductOS can become the PM operating system for both structured state and stakeholder communication if it generates readable discovery, strategy, roadmap, and PRD documents from the same source data.

## Risks And Unknowns

- Human-readable documents could drift from structured artifacts unless document identity and synchronization rules are explicit.
- External publication targets such as SharePoint and Confluence need a stable metadata and versioning model.

## Recommended Next Step

Promote the product document system into the next major upgrade so discovery, strategy, roadmap, prototype, and PRD communication become first-class outputs.
